class Solution {
public:
    int getKth(int lo, int hi, int k) {
        vector<pair<int, int>> vec;
        for (int x = lo; x <= hi; ++x) {
            int steps = getSteps(x);
            vec.push_back({steps, x});
        }
        sort(vec.begin(), vec.end(), [](const auto& a, const auto& b) {
            if (a.first != b.first) return a.first < b.first;
            return a.second < b.second;
        });
        return vec[k-1].second;
    }
private:
    static int getSteps(int x) {
        static unordered_map<int, int> cache = {{1, 0}};
        if (cache.find(x) != cache.end()) {
            return cache[x];
        }
        int steps;
        if (x % 2 == 0) {
            steps = 1 + getSteps(x/2);
        } else {
            steps = 1 + getSteps(3 * x + 1);
        }
        cache[x] = steps;
        return steps;
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    int getKth(int lo, int hi, int k) {
        vector<pair<int, int>> nums;
        for (int x = lo; x <= hi; ++x) {
            int steps = getSteps(x);
            nums.push_back({steps, x});
        }
        sort(nums.begin(), nums.end(), [](const pair<int, int>& a, const pair<int, int>& b) {
            if (a.first != b.first) return a.first < b.first;
            return a.second < b.second;
        });
        return nums[k-1].second;
    }
private:
    static int getSteps(int x) {
        static unordered_map<int, int> cache = {{1, 0}};
        if (cache.find(x) != cache.end()) {
            return cache[x];
        }
        int steps;
        if (x % 2 == 0) {
            steps = 1 + getSteps(x / 2);
        } else {
            steps = 1 + getSteps(3 * x + 1);
        }
        cache[x] = steps;
        return steps;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <algorithm>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int getKth(int lo, int hi, int k) {
        vector<pair<int, int>> nums;
        for (int x = lo; x <= hi; ++x) {
            int steps = getSteps(x);
            nums.push_back({steps, x});
        }
        sort(nums.begin(), nums.end(), [](const auto& a, const auto& b) {
            if (a.first != b.first) return a.first < b.first;
            return a.second < b.second;
        });
        return nums[k-1].second;
    }
private:
    static int getSteps(int x) {
        static unordered_map<int, int> cache = {{1, 0}};
        if (cache.find(x) != cache.end()) {
            return cache[x];
        }
        int steps;
        if (x % 2 == 0) {
            steps = 1 + getSteps(x / 2);
        } else {
            steps = 1 + getSteps(3 * x + 1);
        }
        cache[x] = steps;
        return steps;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N log N)
space complexity: O(M)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses memoization to efficiently compute the power of each number in the range [lo, hi]. It then sorts the numbers based on their power values and numerical value, achieving optimal time complexity through sorting and caching. The main weakness is potential high memory usage for large input ranges, but this is mitigated by the problem constraints.
